stories.headline: Oil holds near multi-month highs as Iran-deal uncertainty and OPEC+ supply decision sustain the war premium
stories.summary: Crude held near multi-month highs into June 3, with WTI settling around $96 and Brent near $98 — each up roughly 2% on the day — as Israeli and US officials signaled readiness for renewed strikes on Iran and US crude inventories posted a sixth straight weekly draw. The bid reflects persistent uncertainty over a stalled Iran ceasefire and Israel's Lebanon operation, with OPEC+ producers due to meet June 7 to set July output.

perspectives.keyFraming: A geopolitical risk premium stays embedded as escalation rhetoric and falling US inventories keep crude bid near multi-month highs.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported WTI settling near $96 and Brent near $98 on June 3 as Netanyahu and Trump signaled willingness for further military action against Iran and EIA data showed a sixth consecutive weekly crude draw, the largest since February. With seven OPEC+ producers due to set July output on June 7, the market continued hedging against an Iran-deal collapse and a wider Lebanon war.

sources.title: Oil prices rise after Netanyahu tells CNBC Israel and U.S. prepared to attack Iran again if necessary

stories.headline: Oil jumps and US stocks slip after Iran halts US talks and threatens to fully close the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Crude held near multi-month highs on June 2 — Brent around $94-95 and WTI near $90-92 — after a roughly 7% Monday surge driven by Iranian reports that Tehran suspended US communications and could fully close the Strait of Hormuz. Prices remain well above pre-conflict levels but below the late-April peak, whipsawing on each diplomatic headline.

perspectives.keyFraming: Supply-disruption risk dominates: Hormuz carries roughly a fifth of seaborne oil, so closure threats alone move prices.
perspectives.summary: CNBC and market-data desks emphasize the physical-supply stakes of a Hormuz shutdown after Iran suspended US talks, framing the spike as a genuine disruption premium. Trading data showed Brent near $94-95 on June 2 after Monday's surge, with prices reversing on every ceasefire or escalation headline.

sources.title: Oil slides on US-Iran near-deal report as Strait of Hormuz stays disrupted

stories.headline: Alphabet prices upsized ~$84.75 billion equity raise for AI build-out, with Berkshire Hathaway anchoring $10 billion
stories.summary: Alphabet priced an upsized equity raise of about $84.75 billion on June 3 — lifted from the roughly $80 billion announced a day earlier after demand overwhelmed terms — to fund AI compute infrastructure. Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway anchored the deal with a $10 billion private placement, split between Class A and Class C shares, in one of the largest tech equity raises on record.

perspectives.keyFraming: Mechanics: a cash-rich Google tapping equity markets signals how capital-intensive the AI race has become.
perspectives.summary: Bloomberg, CNBC and StreetInsider report Alphabet upsized and priced the raise to about $84.75 billion on June 3 — including an at-the-market program, common and depositary shares — after the demand that followed its June 2 announcement, with Berkshire Hathaway taking a $10 billion private placement across Class A and Class C shares. It ranks among the largest tech equity raises ever for a company that rarely issued stock since its 2004 IPO.

sources.title: Alphabet prices $84.75 billion equity raise for AI infrastructure expansion

stories.headline: US stocks end nine-day record streak as Iran escalation lifts oil and pressures megacap tech
stories.summary: Wall Street snapped a nine-session run of record closes on June 3: the S&P 500 fell 0.74% to 7,553.68, the Nasdaq dropped 0.89% to 26,853.98 and the Dow lost 1.21% to 50,687.07. A renewed US-Iran flare-up — Iranian strikes on Kuwait and Bahrain and US strikes on Qeshm Island — pushed oil and Treasuries higher, with Nvidia and Microsoft each falling more than 3% as investors trimmed richly valued AI positions.

perspectives.keyFraming: Geopolitics interrupts a record-setting AI rally as a Middle East risk premium returns.
perspectives.summary: Coverage from Yahoo Finance and The Motley Fool reported record tech momentum giving way to risk-off trading that ended at the close on June 3, snapping a nine-day S&P 500 win streak. The benchmark settled at 7,553.68 after first topping 7,600 intraday, as a Middle East risk premium lifted oil and rotated investors into Treasuries and concentrated losses in megacap tech.

sources.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as oil rises amid US-Iran uncertainty
Stock Market Today, June 3: Markets End Winning Streak on Middle East Escalation Fears

perspectives.keyFraming: Frames the drop as stretched valuations meeting a genuine supply shock after blistering May gains.
perspectives.summary: TheStreet notes that after sharp May advances, richly valued chip stocks had little room for disappointment, so a real energy supply shock from the Iran standoff exposed froth and triggered a pullback led by the Nasdaq.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 3, 2026): Nasdaq, Dow decline from recent records amid tech slide

stories.headline: Broadcom posts record AI-driven Q2 but shares slide after-hours as Q3 chip outlook misses elevated hopes
stories.summary: Broadcom reported fiscal Q2 revenue of about $22.2 billion, up roughly 48% year over year, with AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion (up 143%) and adjusted EPS of $2.44 after the June 3 close. Despite guiding Q3 revenue above estimates and projecting Q3 AI-chip revenue up more than 200% to about $16 billion, shares fell as much as 8% after-hours as the AI figure undershot the highest buy-side expectations following a sharp pre-earnings run-up.

perspectives.keyFraming: Even blowout AI growth isn't enough: Broadcom's guide topped formal estimates but missed elevated buy-side hopes, exposing how priced-for-perfection AI names have become.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported Broadcom's AI semiconductor revenue jumping 143% to $10.8 billion and a Q3 AI guide of roughly $16 billion (up more than 200%), yet shares fell after-hours because the figure landed below the buy-side's higher whisper number. Analysts framed it as a valuation re-rating after a parabolic pre-earnings run rather than a demand problem.

sources.title: Broadcom (AVGO) earnings report Q2 2026
Broadcom Inc. Announces Second Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Financial Results and Quarterly Dividend

perspectives.keyFraming: Demand is real but expectations are unhinged: Broadcom shows custom-silicon AI demand while proving how little 'beat' room priced-for-perfection AI stocks have left.
perspectives.summary: Market commentary framed the after-hours drop as a reality check on AI-hardware valuations rather than a demand issue, tying Broadcom's slide to the broader megacap-tech wobble on June 3 in which Nvidia and Microsoft each fell more than 3% during the regular session.

sources.title: Broadcom Q2 Net Profit Surges 88% YoY, But Shares Slump 8% After Hours

stories.headline: US stock futures slip again as Broadcom sinks and CrowdStrike disappoints, extending the Iran-driven pullback
stories.summary: After Wall Street snapped a nine-day record streak on June 3 (S&P 500 -0.74% to 7,553.68; Nasdaq -0.89%; Dow -1.21%), US equity futures pointed lower again early June 4, with S&P 500 futures roughly -0.4% and Nasdaq-100 futures about -0.6%. Broadcom traded around 13% lower after its AI-chip guide undershot elevated hopes, and CrowdStrike fell about 10% after results, as renewed US-Iran tensions kept oil and Treasury yields elevated.

perspectives.keyFraming: A one-two earnings disappointment compounds the Iran-driven risk-off, dragging futures lower despite strong underlying results.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported S&P 500 futures down about 0.4% and Nasdaq-100 futures about 0.6% early June 4 after the index snapped its nine-session record run. Broadcom slid roughly 13% on a guide that undershot whisper numbers and CrowdStrike fell about 10% even after raising full-year guidance, illustrating how priced-for-perfection AI and security names react to anything short of a blowout.

sources.title: S&P 500 futures fall after index snaps 9-day win streak amid rising Middle East tensions: Live updates

perspectives.keyFraming: Froth meets fundamentals: blistering year-to-date runs left no margin for error, so even strong quarters drew profit-taking.
perspectives.summary: Market commentary framed the slides as valuation re-ratings after parabolic runs rather than demand problems, noting CrowdStrike entered earnings up roughly 64% year-to-date and still fell on a record net-new-ARR quarter, mirroring Broadcom's after-hours drop the prior evening.

sources.title: CrowdStrike (CRWD) Q1 2027 Earnings Transcript

stories.headline: CrowdStrike posts record Q1 net-new ARR and raises guidance, but shares drop about 10% on priced-in expectations
stories.summary: CrowdStrike reported fiscal Q1 2027 revenue of $1.39 billion, up 26% — a fourth straight quarter of acceleration — and a record Q1 net-new ARR of $256 million, up 32%. It raised full-year revenue guidance to about $5.92-$5.96 billion. Despite the beat-and-raise, shares fell roughly 9-10% in reaction, with the stock having run up about 64% year-to-date into the print.

perspectives.keyFraming: A beat-and-raise wasn't enough: a richly valued security leader sold off because expectations had already run ahead of even strong results.
perspectives.summary: Total revenue rose 26% to $1.39 billion and net-new ARR hit a Q1 record $256 million (up 32%), with full-year revenue guided to about $5.9 billion. The roughly 10% drop is attributed to a stock that had already priced in acceleration after a 64% year-to-date run, echoing the Broadcom reaction the same evening.

sources.title: CrowdStrike (CRWD) Q1 2027 Earnings Transcript
CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. — Form 8-K (Q1 FY2027 results)

stories.headline: US-Iran strikes near the Strait of Hormuz push oil toward $100 and pull stocks off record highs
stories.summary: A fragile US-Iran ceasefire frayed further as both sides traded missile and drone attacks. Iranian strikes hit Kuwait — killing at least one and shutting its main airport — while US Central Command said it conducted defensive strikes near the Strait of Hormuz. Brent traded near $100 and WTI in the mid-$90s as US equities retreated from records and Treasury yields rose.

perspectives.keyFraming: Renewed strikes after a collapsed ceasefire reignite supply-disruption fears, pricing a geopolitical risk premium into oil and bonds.
perspectives.summary: Markets coverage framed June 3-4 as a dangerous escalation: Iranian attacks on Kuwait and US strikes near Hormuz revived fears over the roughly fifth of global oil and LNG transiting the strait. Oil rose toward $100, Treasuries sold off with the 10-year near 4.49%, and the S&P 500 and Dow retreated from records as investors weighed how long the conflict could drag on.

sources.title: Stocks slip and oil jumps on fresh US-Iran clashes: Markets Wrap

perspectives.keyFraming: Regional outlets emphasize the conflict spreading to Gulf neighbors and stalled diplomacy as Iran threatens to fully close Hormuz.
perspectives.summary: Al Arabiya and RFE/RL emphasized the conflict spreading to Gulf neighbors — Iranian attacks on Kuwait and US defensive strikes in southern Iran and on Qeshm Island — and stalled diplomacy as Iran halted talks and threatened to fully close the Strait of Hormuz, keeping a risk premium on crude.

sources.title: Iran attacks Kuwait, US strikes near Hormuz as diplomacy stalls
Iran, US exchange attacks as tensions in Gulf rise

stories.headline: ADP shows 122,000 private jobs added in May, lifting Treasury yields ahead of Friday’s payrolls report
stories.summary: ADP reported private payrolls rose 122,000 in May — the strongest since early 2025 and above the roughly 110,000 consensus — with gains across most sectors and annual pay growth holding at 4.4%. The 10-year Treasury yield climbed to about 4.49% on the data amid Iran-driven inflation concerns. The BLS nonfarm payrolls report is due June 5.

perspectives.keyFraming: A resilient labor market plus war-driven inflation pushes yields higher and dims near-term rate-cut hopes.
perspectives.summary: ADP's 122,000 beat showed broad-based hiring and steady 4.4% pay growth, reinforcing a cooling-but-resilient labor market. The 10-year rose to about 4.49% and the 30-year sits near multi-decade highs on inflation concerns tied to the Iran war, as markets await the BLS payrolls report on June 5.

sources.title: ADP jobs report May 2026: Payrolls increase by 122,000

perspectives.keyFraming: Private-sector hiring beats forecasts, but elevated yields and war-driven inflation underscore the cost of the energy shock.
perspectives.summary: Fox Business highlighted the upside surprise and resilient small-business hiring while noting elevated Treasury yields and inflation pressures complicate the rate outlook under the new Fed chair.

sources.title: Private sector adds 122,000 jobs in May, above expectations, ADP says

stories.headline: Dow closes at a record on a rotation into financials; futures soften into the May jobs report as chips drag
stories.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at a record near 51,562 on June 4, up about 1.7%, as investors rotated out of chipmakers into financials, health care and consumer staples; the S&P 500 edged up about 0.4% to around 7,584 while the Nasdaq slipped after Broadcom fell roughly 15%. Heading into Friday, futures pointed lower — S&P futures off about 0.6% — with chip weakness persisting as traders awaited the May jobs report due 8:30 a.m. ET.

perspectives.keyFraming: A record Dow masks a narrowing market as money rotates out of high-multiple AI names into cyclicals ahead of jobs data.
perspectives.summary: Centrist coverage frames the session as a broadening of breadth, with financials and health care leading the Dow to a record even as a Broadcom revenue miss dragged the Nasdaq lower. Into Friday, futures eased and chip weakness lingered as investors positioned for the May payrolls print.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 5, 2026): Nasdaq futures fall on chip stock weakness
Stock market today: Live updates

perspectives.keyFraming: A record Dow shows the economy outside megacap chips is healthy and the rotation is rational.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning framing reads the record Dow as evidence the broader economy is resilient, treating the rotation out of semiconductors as ordinary profit-taking rather than a sign of weakness.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 4, 2026): Broadcom earnings stoke semiconductor selloff

stories.headline: Oil holds a war premium near $95 Brent as Iran ceasefire talks drag and OPEC+ prepares to meet June 7
stories.summary: Brent crude traded around $95 a barrel into June 5 (WTI near $91), still up more than 4% on the week as US-Iran negotiations showed limited progress and Israel's Lebanon operation complicated diplomacy. The EIA reported a sixth straight weekly draw in US crude inventories. OPEC+ ministers meet June 7 to set output, while US pump prices held elevated near $4.24 a gallon.

perspectives.keyFraming: Oil trades on geopolitics, not fundamentals; a durable ceasefire could unwind the premium while OPEC+ adds a wildcard.
perspectives.summary: Centrist coverage attributes the elevated price chiefly to Strait of Hormuz risk and stalled diplomacy, with falling US inventories adding a floor. A signed memorandum could push Brent back toward the $80s, while the June 7 OPEC+ decision is flagged as the next swing factor.

sources.title: Oil slides on US-Iran near-deal report as Strait of Hormuz stays disrupted

perspectives.keyFraming: Domestic inventory draws and US production are cushioning the shock, arguing for more output.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning framing stresses energy security, pointing to falling US crude inventories and domestic production as buffers against the war premium and an argument for expanded drilling and export capacity.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 4, 2026): oil eases as Iran war concerns ebb

stories.headline: Bitcoin slides toward $65,000 support as spot-ETF outflows hit a 2026 record
stories.summary: Bitcoin traded near multi-week lows around $64,000-$65,000 into June 5, down roughly 21% from its mid-May high, pressured by a record run of US spot-ETF redemptions of about $4.4 billion over 13 straight sessions led by BlackRock's IBIT. A bitcoin sale by corporate holder Strategy (MicroStrategy) further dented sentiment. Traders cite higher bond yields, sticky inflation and reduced odds of Fed cuts as the macro headwinds.

perspectives.keyFraming: Crypto is trading as a high-beta risk proxy, not a wartime safe haven.
perspectives.summary: Centrist coverage frames the slide as crypto behaving like a leveraged risk asset during the war, with ETF outflows and liquidations amplifying a broad macro risk-off move rather than acting as a hedge.

sources.title: Bitcoin Falls as Record ETF Outflows and Strategy Sale Hit Sentiment
Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Thursday, June 4, 2026: prices plunge below pre-war levels

perspectives.keyFraming: Institutional selling is a healthy shakeout, not a break in the thesis.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning framing reads the ETF and Strategy selling as a flush of leverage and weak hands rather than a structural breakdown, expecting the asset to stabilize once liquidations clear.

sources.title: Current price of Bitcoin for June 3, 2026

stories.headline: Markets price out Fed cuts ahead of the May jobs report as a hawkish rate path takes hold
stories.summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics releases the May employment report on June 5 at 8:30 a.m. ET, with economist consensus clustered around 80,000-105,000 nonfarm payrolls and unemployment seen holding at 4.3%; Wednesday's ADP print showed private payrolls up 122,000. With energy-driven inflation elevated, markets now price less than a 3% chance of any 2026 rate cut and some desks see rising odds of a hike later in the year. The 10-year Treasury yield sat near 4.48% on Thursday.

perspectives.keyFraming: A cooling-but-not-collapsing labor market hands the Fed a hard tradeoff between sticky inflation and a softening job market.
perspectives.summary: Payroll growth has decelerated (April +115k) without a spike in unemployment — a 'low-hire, low-fire' market. With inflation lifted by the Iran-war energy premium, the Fed is widely expected to hold through 2026, and a hot print could revive hike chatter ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC meeting.

sources.title: The May jobs report will be released Friday. Here's what to expect

perspectives.keyFraming: Inflation discipline is a needed reset; rates should stay tight until prices are tamed.
perspectives.summary: Supporters of a hawkish Fed frame keeping rates high as restoring credibility after years of perceived overreach, arguing a still-positive jobs trend gives policymakers room to prioritize price stability over premature easing.

sources.title: New Fed chair sworn in as inflation worries raise the volume on possible rate hikes

perspectives.keyFraming: A hawkish Fed risks choking a fragile labor market to fight supply-driven inflation it cannot control.
perspectives.summary: Critics warn that holding tight against energy-driven inflation punishes workers and households for a geopolitical shock, and that the politically charged shift at the Fed raises questions about its independence.

sources.title: What Kevin Warsh's Confirmation Hearing Revealed About the Future of the Fed

stories.headline: US adds 172,000 jobs in May, far above forecasts; unemployment holds at 4.3% as Fed-hike bets firm
stories.summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported May nonfarm payrolls rose 172,000, roughly double the ~80K consensus, with unemployment steady at 4.3% and average hourly earnings up 0.3% on the month. Gains concentrated in leisure and hospitality, local government and health care. The hot print pushed traders to price in a possible Fed rate hike by year-end ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC meeting.

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as a 'good news is bad news' print: a resilient labor market that complicates the case for rate cuts and revives hike talk.
perspectives.summary: Business coverage led on the 172,000 payroll gain beating every forecast and unemployment holding at 4.3%, noting wage growth and the shift in rate-path expectations toward a possible 2026 hike ahead of the June FOMC.

sources.title: Jobs report May 2026: Payrolls rose 172,000, topping expectations; unemployment at 4.3%
U.S. added 172,000 nonfarm jobs in May, outpacing forecasts

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as vindication of the administration's economic agenda, with strong hiring touted as growth without runaway inflation.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning framing emphasized the blowout headline number as evidence the economy is accelerating under Trump, casting the report as a midterm-year win even as it pressures the Fed.

sources.title: Trump: Strong jobs report boosts stocks, not inflation

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed cautiously: a long-stalled job market finally adding workers, but with a hawkish Fed threatening to choke the recovery.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning coverage welcomed signs of life after months of weak hiring while warning that a hot print pushing the Fed toward hikes could undercut households still squeezed by elevated prices.

sources.title: Job market finally shows signs of life, adds 172,000 jobs in May

stories.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq slide as strong jobs data fuels Fed-hike fears and the chip selloff deepens
stories.summary: After the hot May jobs report, the S&P 500 fell about 1.6%, the Nasdaq dropped over 2.5% and the Dow slipped roughly 0.7% on June 5. A rotation out of semiconductors accelerated, with Marvell, Micron, AMD and Broadcom all sharply lower, as traders moved to fully price a Fed hike by year-end even as Trump pushes for cuts.

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as a rates-driven risk-off session, with rising yields after the jobs report punishing rate-sensitive tech and chip names hardest.
perspectives.summary: Market coverage attributed the selloff to the hot jobs print lifting Treasury yields and Fed-hike odds, triggering a deep rotation out of semiconductors led by Marvell, Micron, AMD and Broadcom while the broader indexes fell.

sources.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq fall as chips sell off; yields rise after jobs report
Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq sink as jobs report fuels Fed hike bets, chip stocks sell off

stories.headline: Bitcoin slides below $63K, down roughly 14% on the week amid record ETF outflows and risk-off selling
stories.summary: Bitcoin fell to around $62,000 by mid-morning ET on June 5, down about 14% on the week amid roughly $4.4 billion of spot-ETF outflows over 13 sessions. Ether dropped near $1,670 and Solana led losses among major tokens. The hot jobs report, rising Treasury yields and the rejected Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire drove broad risk-off selling.

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as a macro-driven crypto rout: higher yields and geopolitical risk accelerating sustained spot-ETF outflows.
perspectives.summary: Crypto-market coverage tied bitcoin's drop below $63,000 and a roughly 14% weekly loss to record ETF outflows, the hot jobs report and rising yields, with ether and Solana underperforming in a broad risk-off move.

sources.title: Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, June 5, 2026: Prices continue their descent
Crypto Market Today: BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, SOL Extend Losses as Selloff Deepens

stories.headline: Nasdaq sinks ~4% in worst day since April 2025 as Broadcom's soft AI outlook triggers a chip-stock rout
stories.summary: The Nasdaq fell about 4% to 25,709, the S&P 500 dropped 2.6% and the Dow lost roughly 695 points as a semiconductor selloff wiped out hundreds of billions in market value. The slide was triggered by Broadcom, which reported 143% AI-revenue growth but guided next-quarter AI revenue below expectations, sinking its stock about 13% and dragging Nvidia, AMD, Micron and Arm lower. Hot jobs data and rising Treasury yields amplified the move.

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as a single guidance miss erasing roughly $1 trillion, exposing how stretched and concentrated the AI-driven market has become.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning coverage led on Broadcom's outlook falling short of investors' AI-fueled expectations, arguing the violent reaction shows how dependent the market has grown on a handful of richly valued AI names.

sources.title: Broadcom Outlook Falls Short of Investor Expectations for AI Revenue Growth

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as Broadcom's strong-but-not-blowout AI outlook puncturing lofty expectations and detonating a broad semiconductor selloff.
perspectives.summary: Market coverage detailed the Nasdaq's roughly 4% drop — its worst day since April 2025 — and tied it to Broadcom's guidance, with chipmakers Nvidia, AMD, Micron and Arm falling and a hot jobs report and higher yields adding pressure.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 5, 2026): Nasdaq falls 4% as semiconductor slide wipes $1T from markets
Nasdaq falls 4% and suffers worst day since April 2025 as traders flee chip stocks

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as a healthy reality check for a priced-for-perfection chip sector, not a break in the AI thesis.
perspectives.summary: Market commentary noted that even 143% AI revenue growth was not enough for a sector priced for perfection, casting the selloff as a long-overdue correction in overheated semiconductor valuations rather than a thesis-breaking event.
